name: Thought Foundry
description:
Thought Foundry Entertainment PaaS platform founded in 2012, has 11-50 employees, and no funding. Foundry, a 3D design SaaS, founded in 1996, has 300 employees and $46M revenue.

Thought Foundry Entertainment offers PaaS for entertainment, leveraging generative AI for content creation and personalized experiences. AI-powered tools enhance efficiency and creativity. Generative AI drives innovation in storytelling and immersive media.

name: Dash
description:
Dash merged with S4i to form SMRTR in 2025. SMRTR offers automation and compliance solutions. The company was founded in 1998.

Dash AP has merged with AI features, now offering generative AI-powered intelligent automation and document processing solutions. These solutions leverage large language models for enhanced productivity and data extraction.

name: Champ
description:
Champ Software, founded in 1985, employs 11-50 staff and specializes in public health EHR systems. It has over 145 community health agencies as clients. No funding details are available.

Champ Public Health EHR supports public health agencies with population-level data and AI features for decision support and resource optimization. AI enhances public health data modernization and disease surveillance. Generative AI improves health technology assessment and data processing.

name: AutoTime
description:
AutoTime A&D Payroll specializes in payroll and time tracking for aerospace and defense contractors. The company was founded in 2000 and has not disclosed its headcount, revenue, funding, or valuation.

AI-powered payroll systems integrate time tracking and HR software to automate payroll processing, reduce errors, and scale with business growth. ADP Assist uses generative AI for smarter payroll and HR analytics, and Atto streamlines payroll with real-time time tracking integration.

name: Spokane
description:
Spokane Produce, founded in 2000, is a B2B wholesale distributor of fresh produce, deli, and floral products with 183 employees as of 2020. It has raised $114 million in funding and operates in the competitive food distribution market.

The ERP system for fresh produce distribution uses AI for inventory management, order processing, and demand forecasting to reduce waste and optimize operations. It leverages machine learning to handle complex factors like perishability and seasonality. The syste

name: FMSI
description:
Financial Management Software, Inc. (FMSI) is a software development company founded in 1990 with 11-50 employees, specializing in banking operations optimization. FMSI offers scheduling and analytics tools for banks and credit unions. No funding details are available.

name: Primate
description:
Primate Technologies designs SCADA-integrated control room software. Founded in 2000, it employs around 50 staff and has not disclosed revenue or funding details.

Primate Tech offers control room software integrating SCADA and AI for real-time data visualization. Their GridGuardian™ provides unified situational awareness. AI-powered tools like eGridGPT enhance grid operations.

name: ThingTech
description:
ThingTech, founded in 2014, raised $7 million in funding and has around 18 employees. It specializes in IoT asset tracking and management solutions. The company's valuation is not publicly disclosed.

ThingTech is an IoT platform for asset tracking, raising $4 million for growth, and leveraging AI for predictive maintenance.

name: Track Star
description:
Track Star, founded in 2000, offers GPS tracking, telematics, and asset management. It has 10 employees and raised funding from Solen Software Group. The company's valuation is 25/100.

Track Star offers 25-year-old GPS tracking, video telematics, EAM, and AI-powered predictive maintenance. It integrates fleet management, asset tracking, and compliance tools. The platform scales for large fleets and government agencies.

Track Star offers 25-year-old GPS tracking, telematics, and EAM solutions.

name: ViaPeople
description:
ViaPeople, founded in 2002, specializes in performance management and has 11-50 employees. It offers revenue in the $25M-$50M range. The company is based in Plainsboro, New Jersey, and Salt Lake City, Utah.

ViaPeople offers AI-powered performance management, 360-degree feedback, and succession planning software since 2002. They focus on customized, user-friendly solutions. Their AI features enhance insights and efficiency.

name: TrackIt Transit
description:
TrackIt is a paperless transit operations platform founded in 2013 with over 100 transit locations, 64 employees, and an estimated revenue of $17.7M.

TrackIt Transit is a SaaS platform for transit agencies, offering paperless operations, compliance management, and AI-driven features for safety and efficiency. It supports over 100 transit locations and includes modules for attendance, claims, and compliance.

name: Cairn Applications
description:
Cairn Applications provides a SaaS Box Tracker platform for waste haulers, supporting over 350 customers with AI-powered features. Acquired by Solen Software Group in 2025, it continues to operate autonomously.

Cairn Applications' Box Tracker platform is a SaaS solution for waste haulers, with over 350 customers, using AWS, Azure, GCP, and Kubernetes for its technology stack.

name: SMRTR
description:
SMRTR was formed by the merger of DASH and S4i Systems in 2025. It focuses on automation and compliance for manufacturing and food & beverage sectors. SMRTR has not disclosed its headcount, revenue, funding, or valuation.

name: NexTalk
description:
NexTalk, founded in 2008, provides ADA-compliant communication solutions with around 11-50 employees. The company focuses on video relay interpreting software and has not disclosed its funding details.

NexTalk provides ADA-compliant communication solutions, including video relay interpreting and AI-powered tools for the deaf and hard of hearing. Their products ensure privacy and compliance with federal regulations. NexTalk's solutions include remote interpreting and speech-to-text technology.
